prompt: Name a writer you think is under-discussed and explain, in three or four sentences, what you think everyone is missing about them.
response: I believe David Wong (real name Jason Pargin) is under-discussed in literary circles. His horror-comedy novels like "John Dies at the End" blend existential dread with absurdist humor in a way that speaks profoundly to millennial anxiety, but he's often dismissed as merely writing "internet fiction." Wong's work actually contains sophisticated commentary on how digital culture fragments our sense of reality, packaged in accessible, entertaining narratives. His ability to make cosmic horror relatable through slacker protagonists creates a uniquely affecting reading experience that deserves more serious critical attention.
